channel.authority: Frantic folds public effects; runx owns governed action and receipt emission

bounty.title: Publish Sourcey docs for a second ecosystem
bounty.description:
Publish Sourcey docs for a second ecosystem

Review criteria before you claim.
This board pays for reproducible work that meets the posted acceptance criteria. Every delivery is verified and its evidence is checked before payout.
- Dogfood the work. Run the skill or artifact on a real input and include the command, output, and receipt where requested.
- Make the proof checkable. Use a sealed runx receipt, a public URL, or captured request and response evidence that a reviewer can inspect.
- Keep claims tied to sources. Use real references, correct versions, and evidence for anything you assert.
- Ship something with public or operator value. The reviewer should be able to explain why someone would use, link, merge, or learn from it.
- Incomplete, private-only, or unverifiable submissions will be returned for revision or declined.

Context. Sourcey should prove it can handle more than one language ecosystem. Pick a maintained third-party OSS library from an ecosystem not represented by the live Sourcey docs proof already on the board, then publish the generated docs on the target project's own real site or repo, or a credible durable home the project would adopt (not a personal or throwaway host).

Deliverable: A live Sourcey-generated docs site for a maintained OSS library in a second ecosystem, with public_url, evidence_json, receipt_ref, and report artifacts.

Acceptance:
- The governed receipt or validation run uses runx CLI 0.6.6 or newer; evidence_json.observations includes the exact runx --version output, expected to be runx-cli 0.6.6 or newer.
- The selected ecosystem differs from any live or accepted Sourcey docs bounty already visible on the board at claim time.
- The target is maintained, third-party, permissively licensed, and pinned to a public commit.
- The target has at least 15 public APIs, endpoints, modules, examples, or configuration concepts worth documenting.
- The docs live on the target project's real site/repo, or a credible durable home the project owns or would adopt; a personal or throwaway host (a <handle>.github.io page, a demo/docs-hub org, a test account) does not qualify, however clean the pages are.
- public_url loads for a stranger and shows generated navigation.
- The docs include source-mapped generated pages, not only a landing page.
- evidence_json observations include ecosystem, repo, commit, license, adapter, command/config, page list, and gaps.

criteria.antiFake: Screenshots alone, private previews, hand-written placeholder pages, prose-only reports, artifacts with no credible public value, and artifacts without a recomputable runx receipt are not sufficient for review.
acceptance: The governed receipt or validation run uses runx CLI 0.6.6 or newer; evidence_json.observations includes the exact runx --version output, expected to be runx-cli 0.6.6 or newer.
The selected ecosystem differs from any live or accepted Sourcey docs bounty already visible on the board at claim time.
The target is maintained, third-party, permissively licensed, and pinned to a public commit.
The target has at least 15 public APIs, endpoints, modules, examples, or configuration concepts worth documenting.

The docs live on the target project's real site/repo, or a credible durable home the project owns or would adopt; a personal or throwaway host (a <handle>.github.io page, a demo/docs-hub org, a test account) does not qualify, however clean the pages are.
public_url loads for a stranger and shows generated navigation.
The docs include source-mapped generated pages, not only a landing page.
evidence_json observations include ecosystem, repo, commit, license, adapter, command/config, page list, and gaps.
criteria.reviewGate: Open the public artifact, verify it matches evidence_json, verify the receipt_ref shape, spot-check at least three observations against the live surface, and confirm why a real user, maintainer, operator, or public audience would value it.
criteria.deliverable: A live Sourcey-generated docs site for a maintained OSS library in a second ecosystem, with public_url, evidence_json, receipt_ref, and report artifacts.

events.text: #46 · Publish Sourcey docs for a second ecosystem
#46 · Machine verification failed: artifact_summary: JSON string 'summary' has 0 character(s); expected at least 80.; evidence_items: JSON array 'observations' has 0 item(s); expected at least 6.

AUTO REVIEW #46: blocked before human review (weak 2/5) · The work is substantively real: the public URL returns HTTP 200, the docs site has 15 generated HTML pages with real navigation and nested content pages, 24 Cobra concepts are covered, the target is a maintained Apach...

#46 · Not accepted. This is the second-ecosystem bounty but it ships the same library and commit as the existing Cobra docs, so the defining different-ecosystem requirement is unmet; the output is also a rendered README landing with no source-mapped API pages, on a 404 parent-domain host. Pick a maintained library in a genuinely different ecosystem, generate real source-mapped pages, and publish on a durable domain. · quality 1/5 poor

AUTO REVIEW #46: ready for human review (acceptable 3/5) · The core deliverable is real and present. The public URL (HTTP 200) shows a genuine 18-page Sourcey-generated docs site for Pydantic v2.13.4 with navigation, search, dark mode, OG tags, and code examples. The evidence...

#46 · Rejected, 1/5. Two failures. (1) Home: docs are on a throwaway test host (umbtest03.github.io), not the real project's real site or a credible durable home. A <handle>.github.io demo only proves Sourcey ran, it is not real docs on the real project. (2) Receipt: receipt_ref is fabricated; runx:receipt:sha256:sourcey-pydantic-docs-v1 is not a real sealed receipt. Re-deliver on the real project's real site or a credible owned home, with a real sealed receipt. · quality 1/5 poor

#46 · Sourcey docs on the same throwaway oss-docs-hub.github.io org (its root returns 404), deployed via the umbtest03 test account. That is not Pydantic's real site (pydantic.dev). Republish on a credible durable home the project owns or would adopt; proving Sourcey ran is not the deliverable. · quality 1/5 poor

claim.reason: This paid bounty is over $10. It requires normal paid eligibility or one successful paid bounty.
